title: Lec-42: Class A in IP addressing with Example in HINDI | Classful Addressing | Network Layer

segments.text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers. The topic is Class A in IP addressing.

where we have divided IP Addresses in 5 classes, Class A, B, C, D and E.
The concept of Classful Addressing came around 1980s where all IP Addresses were
divided into 5 classes, Class A, B, C, D and E. If we see before that, before 1980,
the concept was that IP Addresses are of 32 bits. So IP Addresses of 32 bits,
they fixed 8 bits were used for network ID or for network numbers,
at that time, before 1980s, they used network numbers, and the remaining 24 bits
these represented host bits. Host bits or host number.
Means how many hosts in which network was represented before 1980s.
